summary: postcss-minify-selector-parser ships AES-GCM-encrypted payload eval'd via new Function() under a typosquat name
details: Package name 'postcss-minify-selector-parser' impersonates the widely-used 'postcss-selector-parser' (which it lists as a dependency and re-exports through src/selector-parser.js to maintain API cover: `module.exports = require("postcss-selector-parser");`). The package's main module additionally exposes `run()` and `runDefaultDecodedFunction()` from src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js. Those functions AES-GCM-decrypt an opaque hardcoded ciphertext (`DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT` in src/config/defaults.js) using a fallback passphrase 'default-dev-passphrase', then execute the resulting plaintext via `new Function("require", runnable)(require)` at src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:48 — handing the eval'd code the live `require` so it can pull in any Node built-in (fs, child_process, http, os) at will. The payload bytes are opaque to the installer and cannot be audited without executing them. Any consumer that calls the documented `run()` export, or runs `npm test` / `npm start` per the package's own scripts, executes attacker-controlled code with the installer's user privileges. The typosquat name plus legitimate-parser re-export is the cover story; the encrypted-blob-then-Function-eval pipeline is the payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
